summary: Added ShellCheck to the tracked shell-script hygiene surface in `just check`, fixed the direct-capture preflight script's ShellCheck warning, and aligned engineering documentation with the command surface while preserving explicit macOS native skips.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Linux `just check` runs ShellCheck over repository shell scripts including `nix/direct-capture-preflight.sh`.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: `just check` now requires ShellCheck outside the Darwin-native missing-tool skip and runs it over tracked `git ls-files -- '*.sh'` results; `nix develop -c just check` passed in evidence/m4-shellcheck-hygiene-gate/commands.log.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: macOS behavior remains native and explicitly skips ShellCheck only when the tool is unavailable outside Linux/Nix.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: The ShellCheck gate follows the existing Darwin skip pattern: missing ShellCheck on Darwin prints `shellcheck skipped outside Linux/Nix`; otherwise the tool is required and run.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Engineering docs and command surface remain aligned.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: docs/ENGINEERING.md lists ShellCheck in the Linux `nix develop -c just check` hygiene gate.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Evidence records focused ShellCheck and full repo check commands.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-shellcheck-hygiene-gate/commands.log records `nix develop -c shellcheck nix/direct-capture-preflight.sh`, `nix develop -c just check`, and `git diff --check`.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No product runtime, capture, encode, transport, decode, render, presentation, cross-device, or latency claim is made.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-shellcheck-hygiene-gate/notes.md records the hygiene-only boundary and no product claims were added.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node only changes repository hygiene command behavior and documentation.
